recipes.name: Kunu Zaki
recipes.description: Kunu Zaki is a Nigeria drink built around millet or sorghum, ginger, cloves, and sweet potato, and the serving logic of cold with snacks.
recipes.story: Kunu Zaki fills a real AfroKitchen gap for Nigeria, where everyday cooking, celebration plates, street snacks, and local drinks often rely on ingredients that do not show up in generic African recipe lists. This version keeps the method practical while preserving the dish's core identity: millet or sorghum, ginger, cloves, and sweet potato, and a table built around cold with snacks.
recipes.occasion: Best for hot days, hosting, Ramadan or celebration drinks, and non-alcoholic refreshment.
recipes.regional_variations: Kunu Zaki changes by household and market availability. Cooks may adjust the main ingredient, heat level, liquid, or serving starch while keeping the same local flavor logic.
recipes.review_summary: Added in the 100-recipe gap-fill wave after comparing live AfroKitchen representation by country. The recipe keeps the most common structure for Kunu Zaki: millet or sorghum, ginger, cloves, and sweet potato, timed cooking steps, and a serving path that makes sense for Nigeria.
recommended_changes: Taste and adjust salt, chili, and acidity at the end.
Use local produce where available, and keep substitutions close to the original texture.
recipes.official_source_status: No direct ministry recipe page was found for every dish in this batch pass. The recipes are synthesized from multiple African culinary, cultural, dish-index, and beverage references, with local method patterns normalized for AfroKitchen.
recipes.author: AfroKitchen research desk

steps.title: Cook or mix base
steps.instruction: Cook or whisk millet or sorghum with clean water into a smooth light base.

steps.title: Cool safely
steps.instruction: Cool until just warm so the starter is not killed.

steps.title: Ferment
steps.instruction: Stir in ginger, cloves, and sweet potato and ferment loosely covered until lightly sour.

steps.title: Serve fresh
steps.instruction: Sweeten or salt lightly, chill, and serve within a day.

steps.title: Prepare the base
steps.instruction: Rinse or prepare dried hibiscus, then combine with water in a pot.

steps.title: Infuse
steps.instruction: Simmer with ginger and spices until the drink is deeply flavored.

steps.title: Strain and sweeten
steps.instruction: Strain while warm, then sweeten and add citrus.

steps.title: Chill
steps.instruction: Chill completely and serve cold over ice.
